food_name: Montepulciano d'Abruzzo Colline Teramane DOCG Red Wine

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general 'red wine' class (FOODON_03301636). No specific entry exists for Montepulciano d'Abruzzo Colline Teramane DOCG; closest_parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code GRA2V covers red wine generically. No specific Montepulciano d'Abruzzo DOCG entry exists in FoodEx2; closest_parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 35748005 is the closest parent concept for wine as a substance. No DOCG-specific code exists.

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard serving of Montepulciano d'Abruzzo Colline Teramane DOCG red wine is approximately 150 ml (one glass), which is the conventional restaurant pour for a full-bodied Italian red wine.

nutrient_source.title: Wine, table, red — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy, FDC ID 174835
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy entry for generic red table wine used as the closest approved source. Montepulciano d'Abruzzo Colline Teramane DOCG is a full-bodied dry red wine; nutrient profile is consistent with generic red table wine values. Alcohol content of this DOCG wine is typically 13–14% ABV, consistent with USDA red wine reference. Values adapted to reflect typical composition of this wine style.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Foster-Powell K, Holt SHA, Brand-Miller JC; red wine analogous estimate
gi_source.organization: American Journal of Clinical Nutrition (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Montepulciano d'Abruzzo Colline Teramane DOCG. Red wine is classified as a low-GI beverage in the peer-reviewed International GI Tables (Atkinson et al. 2008, Am J Clin Nutr). A GI of approximately 45 is used as a conservative analogous estimate for dry red wine, consistent with published values for red table wine. Glycemic load per 150 ml serving calculated as (45 × 2.6 × 1.5) / 100 = 1.76, rounded to 1.8.

hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit alcohol intake to no more than one standard drink per day, as regular alcohol consumption raises blood pressure; a 150 ml glass of this 13–14% ABV red wine contributes approximately 15–17 g of ethanol, which approaches or exceeds recommended daily limits for hypertensive patients.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Alcohol-induced hypoglycaemia risk and low residual sugar
type2_diabetes.guidance: While this dry red wine has a low glycemic index (~45) and minimal residual sugar (~0.62 g/100 ml), individuals with Type 2 Diabetes on insulin or sulfonylureas must exercise caution as alcohol can mask hypoglycaemia symptoms and impair hepatic glucose output; consumption should be limited to one glass with food and blood glucose should be monitored.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Alcohol caloric load, polyphenol content, and ethanol cardiovascular effects
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Moderate red wine consumption has been associated with polyphenol-related cardioprotective effects in observational studies; however, current clinical guidelines do not recommend initiating alcohol consumption for cardiovascular benefit, and patients with established cardiovascular disease should consult their cardiologist before consuming alcohol, limiting intake to no more than one standard drink per occasion.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Montepulciano d'Abruzzo Colline Teramane DOCG is a prestigious Italian red wine produced exclusively in the Colline Teramane (Teramo Hills) sub-zone of the Abruzzo region in central Italy. It is made primarily from the Montepulciano grape variety and carries the Denominazione di Origine Controllata e Garantita (DOCG) designation, the highest classification in Italian wine law, awarded to Colline Teramane in 2003. The 'Adrano' label by Villa Medoro is a well-regarded producer expression of this appellation. The wine is characterised by a deep, impenetrable dark ruby-red colour with garnet reflections, and an intense aromatic profile featuring black cherry, violets, dried herbs, chocolate, and vanilla — the latter two notes typically derived from oak ageing. On the palate it is full-bodied, velvety, and long-finishing with well-integrated tannins. Alcohol content is typically 13–14% ABV. Nutritionally, red wine provides approximately 85 kcal per 100 ml, with negligible protein (0.07 g), virtually no fat, and modest carbohydrate (2.6 g) primarily as residual sugar. It contains trace B vitamins, potassium (127 mg/100 ml), and iron (0.46 mg/100 ml). The primary caloric contributor is ethanol. The wine has a low glycemic index (~45) due to minimal residual sugar, but alcohol itself poses metabolic risks for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, and cardiovascular disease. Sulphites are present as a preservative and represent the primary allergen concern. This wine is not suitable for halal or vegan diets (fining agents may include animal-derived products). It is served at approximately 16–18°C and pairs with rich meat dishes, aged cheeses, and hearty pasta. A standard restaurant serving is 150 ml.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy generic red table wine (FDC ID 174835), which is the closest approved source. No DOCG-specific nutrient analysis was available in approved databases. GI is an analogous estimate from peer-reviewed International GI Tables for red wine. Confidence is medium rather than high because no exact match for this specific DOCG wine exists in any approved primary source.
notes: 1. Food group corrected from input 'Grains and staples' to 'Beverages' — this is an alcoholic beverage (red wine), not a grain or staple. 2. Fat_g is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for red table wine; fat is naturally zero in wine as it is a fermented grape beverage with no lipid fraction. Saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, and trans_fat_g are all 0.0 confirmed zero per the same source. 3. Dietary_fibre_g is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy; wine contains no dietary fibre. 4. Cholesterol_mg is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy; wine contains no cholesterol. 5. Vitamin_c_mg is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy. 6. Vitamin_a_mcg_rae is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy. 7. Vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy. 8. Vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy. 9. Vitamin_e_mg is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy. 10. Protein_g is 0.07 g/100 ml — not zero; trace protein present per USDA source. 11. GI set to 45 as analogous estimate for dry red wine from peer-reviewed International GI Tables (Atkinson et al., Am J Clin Nutr 2008); gi_category is 'low' per threshold ≤55. Glycemic load per 150 ml serving = (45 × 2.6 × 1.5) / 100 = 1.76, rounded to 1.8. 12. Vegan flag set to false as traditional wine fining may use egg white (albumin), casein, or isinglass; this specific wine's fining status is not confirmed from approved sources. 13. Input cuisine_tags ('Pasta', 'Pizza', 'Chicken Dish') are delivery platform artefacts and are not carried forward as they do not describe this wine. 14. Sentinel value -1 is NOT used in this record; all fields are either confirmed numeric values or 0.0 with justification as noted above.
